poem:
**Entry Level**

Mortarboard perched like a rented smile.  
We step off campus, pockets full of futures,  
into a wind that says *No vacancies*.  
Forty-two point five per cent of us  
carrying degrees like umbrellas  
in a drought.

Click, send, refresh—  
a rosary of applications,  
each rejection politely typed,  
each silence louder than debt.
prompt: You are the poet Simon Armitage. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "US college students: are you about to graduate? We’d like to hear about your job-hunting experience The entry-level job market for US college graduates is the worst it has been since the pandemic, according to an analysis by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. The unemployment rate climbed to about 5.6% at the end of last year from an average of 5.3% during the third quarter. The underemployment rate rose to 42.5% – its highest level since 2020, the research found. We would like to talk to people who are graduating this year. How are you handling the job application process? How many jobs have you applied for? What has been your experience of job hunting so far?". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.

How are you handling the job application process? How many jobs have you applied for? What has been your experience of job hunting so far?
